description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular, stacked-stone base and a wooden support structure holding up a pitched, red-tiled roof. The well features a visible hand crank mechanism with interlocking gears, likely used to raise and lower a bucket for drawing water. The stonework is dark gray and textured, giving the well a sturdy and weathered appearance. The roof is made of overlapping red tiles, providing shelter to the well opening and mechanical parts. The wooden beams supporting the roof are robust and intersect at the center, adding to the rustic charm. The well is open at the top, with no visible cover over the water opening.
description: A rustic stone water well with a red-tiled roof, wooden supports, and a visible hand crank mechanism. The well has a circular stone base and an open top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the hand crank, gears, and the open well beneath the red-tiled roof.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the red-tiled roof, with the crank handle visible on the left and the stone base below.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the wooden support beams and the open well, with no visible mechanical parts.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the crank handle on the right, showing the roof and stone base.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A visible hand crank and gear mechanism is present on the front, made of metal and wood, but no bucket is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well opening; a pitched roof canopy made of red tiles is supported by wooden beams.
